chosen_logo.why: JSON-LD Organization.logo points to the canonical P&G monogram PNG (P_G_Logo_RGB.png) on Contentful CDN — full-resolution 3004×3004 RGB version, transparent background. Visible in every page header.

brand_primary.name: --color-brand-blue
brand_primary.evidence: P&G's signature Pantone 286 blue. Computed body/nav/h2/h3/footer colour across all 6 pages; dominant cover bg on home and innovation pages; literal hex appears 105× in CSS.

brand_secondary.name: --color-brand-blue
brand_secondary.evidence: P&G commits to one saturated brand blue. There is no separate 'navy' — the same Pantone 286 carries both the dominant chord (cover bg) and the accent role (CTA outlines, header nav, h2 type).

ink.name: --color-brand-ink
ink.evidence: Computed body color rgb(0,0,0) on every page. P&G uses pure black for body text — the brand book is unsentimental about typography colour.

paper.name: --color-brand-paper
paper.evidence: White surface for content-dense pages (leadership-team). The blue-bg slides invert: white type on saturated blue. Two-mode design — white surface OR blue surface, never anything in between.

accents.name: --color-brand-lime
accents.evidence: Bright lime green — appears as a sustainability/nature accent in body content. Count 3× in literal CSS.

accents.name: --color-brand-sky
accents.evidence: Sky cyan — secondary brand accent (e.g. Tide-family categorical color). Count 3× in literal CSS.

accents.name: --color-brand-mist
accents.evidence: Pale blue tint for hero backgrounds on innovation page; row separation. Count 19× in literal CSS.

accents.name: --color-brand-deepteal
accents.evidence: Dark teal accent — appears in footer-adjacent contexts.

accents.name: --color-brand-spark
accents.evidence: P&G's signature decorative-period yellow — appears as the coloured stop after large titles ('Innovation.', 'Leadership.'). Embedded in title assets rather than CSS literals; visible in every hero screenshot.

neutrals.name: --color-brand-graphite
neutrals.evidence: Secondary text grey — count 6× across pages.

neutrals.name: --color-brand-stone
neutrals.evidence: Warm grey-brown — minor neutral for citizenship/lifestyle pages.

display_font.evidence: Computed h1/h2/h3 font-family on every page: 'Montserrat, "Montserrat Fallback", sans-serif'. 96 px h1 weight 700 on home, innovation, who-we-are, citizenship — confirms big-billboard treatment.

body_font.evidence: Computed body font on header/nav/footer/main is also Montserrat (the 'Times' that shows on the bare body element is the unstyled fallback for default <body> before P&G's CSS rules apply). Frequencies show Montserrat 59× — the brand uses one face for everything, weight differentiates display vs body.

spacing_radius_shadow.radius_style: split — sharp 0px on surfaces, 50px pill on interactive
spacing_radius_shadow.evidence: Computed surface radius 0px on every header/footer/main/h1/h2 — P&G commits to sharp rectangles for content. CTAs (`a` tags) have radius:50px — full pill. No box-shadow observed on any computed element. Body padY:66px, footer padY:50px — generous vertical rhythm.

precedents: modernist Swiss editorial — but with consumer warmth in the imagery
aesthetic.voice_register: operational confidence; portfolio-led ('Most Innovative for Third Consecutive Year', '#1 Most Innovative in Household Products'); the brand speaks in superlatives that are earned, not aspirational
